title: The code review that actually helps
hook: A good review is not a scavenger hunt for style nits. It is a fast, careful pass over the parts that can hurt users, future readers, or production.

beats.heading: Review the change, not the author

blocks.html: The best reviews have a narrow ego footprint. They ask: does this change solve the right problem, preserve the contract, keep failure modes visible, and leave the next person a path through the code? Everything else is secondary.

blocks.text: The reviewer owns the health of the codebase. The author owns the patch. A useful review helps both.

blocks.caption: A tiny patch rarely touches only one thing. Push it and watch the review surface appear.

blocks.html: Start with the user-facing behavior, public API, data shape, and tests. Then read the implementation. That order catches the real bugs: the endpoint changed shape, the migration misses old rows, the background job retries forever, or the test proves the mock instead of the feature.

blocks.title: zsh · review checklist
blocks.caption: A review pass that stays focused.

blocks.html: A blocker should name the bad outcome and the fix direction. A suggestion should sound like a suggestion. Mixing those two wastes time because the author has to reverse-engineer priority from tone.

blocks.code:
Blocker: this returns 200 after the write fails, so the UI will show
a successful pause even when the database rejected it. Please return
the error path and add a regression test.

Suggestion: the helper name could be clearer, but it does not need
to block this PR.
blocks.caption: Priority is part of the message.

blocks.title: change confidence lab
blocks.caption: Flip the switches and watch the story turn into a tiny operating model.
blocks.low: fragile: this change still depends on hope and a lucky deploy
blocks.mid: better: the main risk has at least one alarm around it
blocks.high: solid: tests, observability, and review all agree on the shape

beats.heading: The fastest review is the one with trust in it

blocks.html: Small PRs, clear tests, and direct comments create speed. Not because people type faster, but because there is less guessing. The review becomes a shared debugging session instead of a courtroom transcript.

items.title: How to do a code review

sources.title: How to do a code review
